justification: All 4 business_impact sub-fields (financial, operational, user, risk) are substantively populated with multi-sentence analysis. stakeholders_affected lists 8 distinct groups with specific impact per group (far exceeding the 3 minimum). Multiple escalations for missing information (SLA contracts, scope of affected shipments, same-day delivery, API impact). Contractual SLA risk is explicitly and thoroughly identified. confidence.level is 52, well under the 80 threshold, with detailed assumptions listed. Competitive positioning is mentioned (industry trend reference to Amazon/FedEx offering time windows).

observations.text: Exceptional stakeholder analysis — 8 distinct groups identified with specific, differentiated impact statements for each
4 well-structured escalations covering SLA contracts, scope ambiguity, same-day delivery, and API breaking changes — each with options and recommendations
Confidence level of 52 is well-calibrated given the missing contract information, with clear separation of high/low confidence areas
Financial analysis correctly identifies the dual nature — reduced refund claims for B2C vs SLA penalty risk for B2B
Operational benefit is properly balanced against customer experience cost rather than presented one-sidedly
Competitive positioning addressed via industry trend reference (Amazon, FedEx moving to time windows)
Edge cases are thorough — timezone handling, midnight boundary, same-day delivery, scheduled time slots, and API breaking changes
Business rules correctly distinguish invariants from policy decisions with clear justifications
